summary: Map nobc-os subsystems for the Sponsor Intelligence build (schema, intelligence routes, AI SDK, DAM share/R2, archetypes/RSVP/check-in, PDF/fonts/tokens, Resend)
result:
Verified: `SponsorBrandProfile` has no rights-fee/objectives fields, `GeneratedAsset` exists with `type String`, and there is **no** `EventSponsor` model. Brief follows.

---

# BUILD BRIEF — Sponsor Intelligence (Phases 0/1/2)

Target repo: `/Users/adamsemien/nobc-os`. Schema is shared with Producer on the same Neon Postgres. **Every change below is additive-only; `prisma db push` is forbidden** (drops `Asset_searchVector_idx`). Workflow for all schema work: edit `prisma/schema.prisma` → `npx prisma generate` → `npx prisma migrate diff --from-schema-datasource prisma/schema.prisma --to-schema-datamodel prisma/schema.prisma --script` → review for any DROP/ALTER TYPE/RENAME → `npx prisma db execute --file <migration.sql>`.

---

## 1. SCHEMA ADDITIONS (additive-only)

### 1a. Sponsor Brief intake — extend EXISTING `SponsorBrandProfile`
There is **no `EventSponsor` model** (confirmed). Sponsor identity lives on `SponsorBrandProfile` (line 1135). Add three nullable columns there — all additive (`ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN`, no enum touch):

```prisma
model SponsorBrandProfile {
  // ... existing: id, workspaceId, name, icp, icpTags, category, primaryColor, logoAssetId,
  //     generatedAssets, seriesSponsors, workspace, createdAt, updatedAt, @@index([workspaceId])
  declaredObjectives    String?   // free-text brief objectives (e.g. "200 founders, 30% scan rate")
  targetPersonaCriteria Json?     // { archetypes: string[], industries: string[], seniority: string[], minAttendance: int }
  rightsFeeCents        Int?      // sponsorship rights fee in cents (money-as-cents convention, matches priceInCents)
}
```
Workspace scope: inherited (`workspaceId` + `@@index([workspaceId])` already present). No new index needed.

### 1b. Recap metrics snapshot (Phase 0) — NEW model `RecapSnapshot`
Reproducible frozen numbers behind a recap PDF (so a regenerated PDF and the magic-link agree). Snapshot is keyed to an event + optional sponsor; numbers stored as Json so we never re-run aggregation against mutated data.

```prisma
model RecapSnapshot {
  id             String   @id @default(cuid())
  workspaceId    String
  workspace      Workspace @relation(fields: [workspaceId], references: [id])
  eventId        String   // soft FK (plain string + index, mirrors Asset.eventId / Event.heroImageAssetId)
  sponsorBrandId String?  // nullable; ties recap to a SponsorBrandProfile
  sponsorBrand   SponsorBrandProfile? @relation(fields: [sponsorBrandId], references: [id])
  metrics        Json     // frozen { attendance, scanRateByTier, archetypeMix, personaMatchPct, fieldAggregations }
  generatedAt    DateTime @default(now())
  generatedBySession String?
  createdAt      DateTime @default(now())

  @@index([workspaceId])
  @@index([eventId])
  @@index([sponsorBrandId])
}
```
Add back-relations on `Workspace` (`recapSnapshots RecapSnapshot[]`) and `SponsorBrandProfile` (`recapSnapshots RecapSnapshot[]`). All additive.

### 1c. SurveyResponse (Phase 1) — NEW model + NEW enum
There is **no `Survey` model** today; event registration answers are loose JSON on RSVP. This is a net-new normalized table. Use a **new `CREATE TYPE` enum** — do NOT `ALTER TYPE` `QuestionFlowStep` or `MemberEngagementEventType`.

```prisma
enum SurveyPhase {   // NEW enum — CREATE TYPE, not ALTER
  PRE
  POST
}

model SurveyResponse {
  id             String      @id @default(cuid())
  workspaceId    String
  workspace      Workspace   @relation(fields: [workspaceId], references: [id])
  eventId        String      // soft FK, plain string + index (matches house pattern)
  sponsorBrandId String?     // optional sponsor attribution
  sponsorBrand   SponsorBrandProfile? @relation(fields: [sponsorBrandId], references: [id])
  memberId       String?     // nullable: guests/comps may answer without a Member row
  member         Member?     @relation(fields: [memberId], references: [id])
  phase          SurveyPhase
  answers        Json        // { questionKey: value }
  submittedAt    DateTime    @default(now())
  createdAt      DateTime    @default(now())

  @@index([workspaceId])
  @@index([eventId])
  @@index([sponsorBrandId])
  @@index([memberId])
}
```
Back-relations: `Workspace.surveyResponses`, `Member.surveyResponses`, `SponsorBrandProfile.surveyResponses`. The task spec said "sponsorId?" — model it as `sponsorBrandId` to match the real FK target (`SponsorBrandProfile.id`); there is no standalone Sponsor table.

### 1d. GeneratedAsset (Phase 0/2 PDFs) — REUSE existing model, NO schema change
`GeneratedAsset` (line 1155) already has exactly what's needed: `type String` (comment: "simple String for now; promote to enum later"), `pdfUrl String`, `magicLinkUrl String @unique`, `payload Json`, `expiresAt DateTime?`, `sponsorBrandId String?`, `workspaceId` + `@@index([workspaceId])`. **Use `type` string values `"activation_recap"` and `"audience_intelligence_brief"`** — do NOT add an enum (that would be `ALTER TYPE` / new column churn for no gain; the field is already `String` by design). `pdfUrl` stores the R2 object key (not a public URL). No migration for 1d.

### HARD-STOP triggers (stop and ask Adam — do NOT auto-apply)
- Any migrate-diff line containing `DROP`, `ALTER TYPE ... ADD VALUE`, `RENAME`, or `ALTER COLUMN ... TYPE`.
- Any urge to add `AT_ACTIVATION` / `AT_CHECKIN` to `QuestionFlowStep`, or any value to `MemberEngagementEventType` (`sponsor_perk_clicked` already exists — use `metadata` Json instead).
- The 5-tier influence mapping (§5) and confirming `declaredObjectives`/`rightsFeeCents` live on `SponsorBrandProfile` (vs. a future per-event sponsor model) are **product decisions** — confirm before building UI on top.
- Embedding commercial fonts (PP Editorial New / Neue Haas Grotesk) into distributable PDFs is a licensing question (§6) — confirm before shipping downloadable sponsor PDFs.

---

## 2. ROUTE DECISION

**Do NOT extend `/api/intelligence/{compose,reports}`.** `compose` is a stateless NL→metric-tile composer (`{question, filters}` → ephemeral `Composition`); `reports` is pure `SavedReport` CRUD with zero AI. A recap is a fixed, event/sponsor-scoped, persisted deliverable — wrong shape for both, and would break `reports`' GET-open/POST-STAFF split.

**Create these new routes/actions** (all workspace-scoped, all `runtime = 'nodejs'` where they touch crypto/R2):

| Path | Verb | Gate | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `app/api/intelligence/activation-recap/route.ts` | POST | `requireRole(STAFF)` | Compute recap numbers for an `eventId` (+`sponsorBrandId?`), persist a `RecapSnapshot`, render PDF → R2, create `GeneratedAsset` (`type:"activation_recap"`) + magic-link token. Returns the share URL. |
| `app/operator/intelligence/sponsor/actions.ts` → add `generateAudienceIntelligenceBrief(workspaceId)` | server action | inherits page `requireRolePage(ADMIN)` — also gate inside if callable elsewhere | Wire behind the **stubbed `SponsorBriefBar` "Generate One-Sheeter"** button. Produces `GeneratedAsset` (`type:"audience_intelligence_brief"`). |
| `app/api/share/links/route.ts` (existing) | POST | `requireRole(STAFF)` | Reuse as-is for share links; or mint via `GeneratedAsset.magicLinkUrl` for PDF-only delivery (see §3). |
| Public delivery: reuse `app/assets/[token]` (SPONSOR) machinery, or `app/doc/[token]/page.tsx` if PDF has no folder | GET | token + cookie | Magic-link landing → password gate → signed PDF download. |

Survey ingest (Phase 1): `app/api/intelligence/survey/route.ts` POST (`requireRole(STAFF)` to create/configure; public submit path mirrors the share token pattern if members submit unauthenticated).

---

## 3. REUSABLE BUILDING BLOCKS (exact imports + calls)

**Sonnet `generateObject`** (structured; locked model) — copy from `lib/scoring.ts`:
```ts
import { anthropic } from '@ai-sdk/anthropic';
import { generateObject } from 'ai';
import { z } from 'zod';
const MODEL = 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514'; // locked — do not change
const ResultSchema = z.object({ summary: z.string().describe('one-line headline'), score: z.number().min(0).max(1) });
if (!process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) return FALLBACK;
try {
  const { object } = await generateObject({ model: anthropic(MODEL), schema: ResultSchema, prompt });
  return object;
} catch (e) { console.error('[activationRecap] generateObject failed:', e); return FALLBACK; }
```

**Haiku `generateText`** (prose) — ONLY valid in `app/operator/intelligence/sponsor/actions.ts` (authorized exception #2). New prose elsewhere must use Sonnet.
```ts
import { anthropic } from '@ai-sdk/anthropic';
import { generateText } from 'ai';
const NARRATIVE_MODEL = 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001'; // AUTHORIZED sponsor-narrative exception only
const { text } = await generateText({
  model: anthropic(NARRATIVE_MODEL), system: SYSTEM_PROMPT,
  prompt: `...instructions...\nSignals:\n${signals}\n\nWrite only the N sentences.`,
  maxOutputTokens: 320, temperature: 0.8,
});
return text.trim(); // and: if (!process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) return '';
```

**R2 private upload** (`lib/dam/storage.ts`):
```ts
import { damKey, uploadObject } from '@/lib/dam/storage';
const key = damKey(workspaceId, generatedAssetId, 'recap.pdf'); // dam/{workspaceId}/{id}/recap.pdf
await uploadObject(key, pdfBuffer, 'application/pdf'); // throws StorageNotConfiguredError if unconfigured
// then persist key to GeneratedAsset.pdfUrl; bump workspace.storageBytes in a db.$transaction
```

**R2 signed/streamed download** (forces save dialog cross-origin):
```ts
import { presignGet, DOWNLOAD_URL_TTL } from '@/lib/dam/storage';
const signed = await presignGet(key, DOWNLOAD_URL_TTL, { downloadFilename: 'recap.pdf' }); // 24h, Content-Disposition: attachment
if (!signed) return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Storage unavailable' }, { status: 503 });
return NextResponse.redirect(signed, { status: 302 });
```

**ShareLink / token create** (`app/api/share/links/route.ts` pattern):
```ts
import { mintShareToken, shareAbsoluteUrl } from '@/lib/share/token';
import { hashPassword } from '@/lib/share/password';
import { ShareLinkMode } from '@prisma/client';
const token = mintShareToken();                       // 32-byte base64url (~256-bit)
const passwordHash = password ? await hashPassword(password) : null; // scrypt, NOT bcrypt
// ShareLink requires non-null folderId + mode; for a folderless PDF use GeneratedAsset.magicLinkUrl instead.
```

**Password-gated HttpOnly cookie — set + verify** (`lib/share/auth-cookie.ts`):
```ts
import { SHARE_AUTH_COOKIE_NAME, SHARE_AUTH_TTL_SECONDS, buildShareAuthCookie, shareAuthCookiePath, verifyShareAuthCookie } from '@/lib/share/auth-cookie';
import { verifyPassword } from '@/lib/share/password';
// SET (auth route, after verifyPassword against a dummy hash for timing-safety):
res.cookies.set({ name: SHARE_AUTH_COOKIE_NAME, value: buildShareAuthCookie(link.id, link.passwordHash),
  httpOnly: true, sameSite: 'lax', secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production',
  path: shareAuthCookiePath(shareUrlPath(link.mode, token)), maxAge: SHARE_AUTH_TTL_SECONDS });
// VERIFY (resolve, server-side):
const ok = verifyShareAuthCookie(store.get(SHARE_AUTH_COOKIE_NAME)?.value ?? null, link.id, link.password);
```

**Resend send** — locked from-address; never parameterize:
```ts
import { resend } from '@/lib/resend';
import { sendTemplatedEmail } from '@/lib/email'; // FROM = 'NoBC <team@thenobadcompany.com>'
// templated (preferred): add a default to DEFAULT_EMAIL_TEMPLATES first, then:
await sendTemplatedEmail(workspaceId, 'sponsor.recap_ready', toEmail, { sponsor:{ name }, recap:{ url } });
// raw (sensitive links, no audit): if (process.env.RESEND_API_KEY) await resend.emails.send({ from:'NoBC <team@thenobadcompany.com>', to, subject, html }).catch(e=>console.error('[recap] email failed:', e));
```

**`requireRole` gate** (`lib/operator-role.ts`):
```ts
import { OperatorRole } from '@prisma/client';
import { requireRole, requireRolePage } from '@/lib/operator-role';
const gate = await requireRole(OperatorRole.STAFF); // route handler
if (!gate.ok) return gate.response;
const { workspaceId, userId } = gate;
// server page: const { workspaceId } = await requireRolePage(OperatorRole.ADMIN); // redirects, doesn't return a response
```

---

## 4. DATA QUERIES (exact Prisma shapes — all `where:{ workspaceId }`)

**Attendance count** (confirmed registrants for an event):
```ts
db.rSVP.count({ where: { workspaceId, eventId, status: 'CONFIRMED' } });
// denominator incl. held: status:'CONFIRMED', ticketStatus:{ in:['confirmed','held'] }
```

**Check-in scan rate by access tier** — tier is DERIVED, not a column:
```ts
const rows = await db.rSVP.findMany({
  where: { workspaceId, eventId },
  select: { isComp: true, compType: true, checkedIn: true, ticketStatus: true, tier: { select: { name: true } },
            member: { select: { status: true } } }, // status only — NOT name/email
});
// bucket: isComp===true -> Comp (kind=compType); else member.status==='APPROVED' -> Member; ==='GUEST' -> Guest
// scanRate(bucket) = count(checkedIn===true) / count(ticketStatus in ['confirmed','held'])
```
Cross-event trend: `db.memberEngagementEvent.findMany({ where:{ workspaceId, eventType:'checked_in' }, select:{ memberId:true, eventId:true, occurredAt:true } })`.

**Archetype / influence-tier distribution** — archetype lives on `Application`, not `Member`:
```ts
const attendees = await db.rSVP.findMany({ where:{ workspaceId, eventId, checkedIn:true },
  select:{ member:{ select:{ email:true } }, guestEmail:true } });
const emails = attendees.map(a => a.member?.email ?? a.guestEmail).filter(Boolean) as string[];
const apps = await db.application.findMany({ where:{ workspaceId, email:{ in: emails } },
  select:{ archetype:true } }); // archetype String? — one of 6 ArchetypeName; null => 'Unsegmented'
// map archetype -> influence tier (§5), then COUNT per tier (never per-person)
```

**Registration-field answer aggregation for persona match**:
```ts
const defs = await db.eventCustomQuestion.findMany({ where:{ workspaceId, eventId },
  select:{ id:true, label:true, fieldType:true, options:true, showToMember:true, showToGuest:true } });
const ans = await db.rSVP.findMany({ where:{ workspaceId, eventId },
  select:{ customAnswers:true, attendeeCustomAnswers:true } }); // loose JSON, parse by question id/label
// aggregate ONLY SELECT/MULTISELECT/CHECKBOX option distributions; NEVER raw TEXT/TEXTAREA values
```

**Suppression + PII rules (enforce at aggregation layer, AFTER all joins):**
- **Under-5 suppression:** any sponsor-facing bucket (by tier, archetype/influence tier, answer option, event) with `< 5` attendees → hide or roll into `"Other"`. Apply to every cross-tab cell so a small bucket can't be reconstructed by subtraction. **Not enforced anywhere today** (`sponsor-fit-score.ts` returns raw counts) — you must add it.
- **NEVER select / emit for sponsors:** `Member.firstName/lastName/email/phone`, `RSVP.guestEmail/guestName/attendeeName/attendeeEmail/attendeePhone`, `Member.redListed`, `Application.aiReasoning/aiSummary/aiTags/reviewNote/rejectionReason`, any REJECTED/DECLINED/HOLD applications, raw free-text registration answers, and per-person firmographics. Firmographics (`industry/jobFunction/seniority/companySize/ageRange/householdIncome`) and `archetype` → aggregate distributions only.
- Strip `__`-prefixed internal tags (e.g. `__demo`) — `loadNetworkCapital` already does this.
- Respect the existing `approvedCount < 5` empty-state threshold on the sponsor page.

---

## 5. ARCHETYPE → INFLUENCE TIER MAP (derived lookup; do NOT edit locked `config/archetypes.ts`)

6 shipped archetypes → 5 requested influence tiers. Store as a lookup over `Application.archetype`; `Host` is the orphan. **Confirm with Adam before building UI** (HARD-STOP product decision):

| Archetype (locked) | → Influence tier | Rationale (from archetype `tags`) |
|---|---|---|
| Connector | **Connector** | network / matchmaker / social-capital (direct) |
| Curator | **Tastemaker** | tags literally `tastemaker, cultural-capital, editorial, discernment` |
| Builder | **Operator** | tags `operator, executor, ship-it` |
| Maker | **Creator** | tags `creative, artist, craftsperson, hands-on` |
| Patron | **Founder** | investor / door-opener / backer ≈ capital tier |
| Host | **Connector** (fold) | room-making/relationship; no clean 5th slot — flag alt: surface Host separately |
| (no archetype / guest / comp) | **Unsegmented** | null `Application.archetype` — keep, don't drop, or counts won't reconcile |

Alt to flag: `Builder→Founder` + `Patron→Operator` if "Operator" should mean capital allocator.

---

## 6. DEPS & ASSETS

**PDF renderer:** NONE installed (no `@react-pdf/renderer`, `pdfkit`, `pdf-lib`, `jspdf`, `puppeteer`). Install `npm i @react-pdf/renderer`. Render server-side via `renderToBuffer`/`renderToStream` on **Node runtime only** (`export const runtime = 'nodejs'`; not edge; may need `serverExternalPackages`).

**Fonts:** PRESENT — both brand families in `/Users/adamsemien/nobc-os/public/fonts/` as `.otf` AND `.ttf` (64 files). Register the **`.ttf`** variants (CFF/.otf support in react-pdf is flaky), each weight/style as a separate `fonts:[{src,fontWeight,fontStyle}]` entry (react-pdf does not synthesize bold/italic). Minimum set:
- `'PP Editorial New'`: `PPEditorialNew-Regular.ttf` (400), `-Italic.ttf` (400 it), `-Bold.ttf` (700) — headings.
- `'Neue Haas Grotesk Display Pro'`: `NHaasGroteskDSPro-55Rg.ttf` (400), `-56It.ttf` (400 it), `-65Md.ttf` (500), `-75Bd.ttf` (700) — body.
- Read via `process.cwd() + '/public/fonts/...'`. **LICENSING HARD-STOP:** these are commercial retail fonts; embedding into distributable PDFs is a separate grant from web `@font-face` — flag to Adam before shipping downloadable sponsor PDFs.

**PDF color palette** (react-pdf `StyleSheet.create` needs literal hex — CSS vars / oklch / color-mix do NOT work; put these in a dedicated `lib/pdf/` constant, NOT scattered in components — the "no hex literals" rule is for themed components):

| Role | Hex | Token source |
|---|---|---|
| Page bg (cream paper) | `#f8f5ef` | `--events-paper` |
| Cream field / alt bg | `#fcfaf6` | `--events-ref-cream` |
| Card surface | `#fefcf7` | `--events-paper-card` |
| Body + heading ink | `#201915` | `--events-ref-ink` |
| Secondary text | `#5b504a` | `--events-ref-muted` |
| Faint / caption | `#90837b` | `--events-ref-faint` |
| Terracotta accent | `#9d5037` | `--events-ref-accent` |
| Hairline rule | `#ddd6cf` | `--events-ref-rule` |
| Brand red | `#b22e21` | `--nobc-red` |
| Text on red | `#fdfcf9` | `--nobc-on-red` |
| Charts: connector/host/curator/builder/maker/patron | `#B22E21` / `#C47A3A` / `#8B6914` / `#2D5A3D` / `#4A3580` / `#1A3A5C` | archetype palette (already literal) |

(Note doc drift: CLAUDE.md cites `#f9f7f2` / `#1a1520`; use the CSS-verified cream-paper values above for the editorial PDF.) Other installed deps usable: `sharp ^0.34.5` (downscale hero images before embed), `@aws-sdk/client-s3` + presigner (R2), `qrcode ^1.5.4` (recap QR).

**Images in PDF:** R2 originals are private — fetch via `presignGet` signed URL or read buffer server-side; `<Image>` needs a buffer/fetchable URL. Pre-rasterize with `sharp`.

---

## 7. TOP RISKS / GOTCHAS

1. **`prisma db push` = production outage** — drops `Asset_searchVector_idx` (GIN, lives only in prod via `prisma/sql/dam-search-vector.sql`) and Producer shares the DB. Additive `migrate diff` + `db execute` only. **HARD STOP** on any DROP/ALTER TYPE/RENAME in the diff.
2. **Model lock:** `claude-sonnet-4-20250514` everywhere. Haiku (`claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`) is authorized **only** in `sponsor/actions.ts` (and SMS). New prose surfaces (recap PDF copy, survey summaries) default to Sonnet unless Adam extends the exception in-comment.
3. **Under-5 suppression is not built anywhere** — you must add it before any external sponsor surface; `sponsor-fit-score.ts` leaks raw counts today.
4. **PII leakage:** `app/api/check-in/event/route.ts` selects names/emails — do NOT reuse its projection. Strip names, contact, redList, raw application reasoning, free-text answers, per-person firmographics.
5. **No `EventSponsor` model** — sponsor attribution is series-level (`SeriesSponsor`) or via `SponsorBrandProfile`. Recap/survey link sponsor via `sponsorBrandId` (FK → `SponsorBrandProfile.id`), and event via soft-FK `eventId String` (no FK). Don't assume per-event sponsor rows exist.
6. **Archetype is on `Application`, not `Member`** — null for guests/comps; bucket as `'Unsegmented'`, never drop (attendance won't reconcile).
7. **Download cap TOCTOU:** `ShareLink.allowedDownloads` check is `count()` then `create()` (non-atomic). Acceptable at NoBC scale; for higher-volume PDF magic links use the atomic `UPDATE ... WHERE downloadCount < allowedDownloads` instead.
8. **Cookie isolation = Path scope + per-share HMAC** — `SHARE_AUTH_COOKIE_NAME` is one shared name; set `path` to the exact doc/share URL prefix or cookies leak between shares. `secure: NODE_ENV==='production'` (not hardcoded `true`) so localhost dev works.
9. **scrypt, not bcrypt** — match `scrypt$N$r$p$salt$hash`; the schema comment saying "bcrypt" is wrong.
10. **Resend from-address locked** to `team@thenobadcompany.com` — never parameterize. Add a `DEFAULT_EMAIL_TEMPLATES` entry (not just a DB row) for any new templated email; keep tokens out of the email **subject** (subject lands in audit metadata). Idempotency is the caller's job (add a `*SentAt` flag like `reminderSentAt`).
11. **Terminology law:** "Access" / "Event Access", never "RSVP" in sponsor-facing copy; humanize all enum values (`TICKETED`, `MemberStatus.GUEST`, archetype enums) before display.
12. **AI no-op gate mandatory:** every Anthropic call guards `if (!process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)` and returns a fallback; every catch `console.error('[feature] ...', e)` (never swallow).
13. **`Workspace.storageBytes` is BigInt** — don't `JSON.stringify` a full Workspace row; serialize carefully when bumping it in the upload transaction.

Files to create: `app/api/intelligence/activation-recap/route.ts`, `app/api/intelligence/survey/route.ts`, `lib/pdf/` (palette + font registration + recap/brief documents), and extend `app/operator/intelligence/sponsor/actions.ts` + `SponsorBriefBar.tsx`. Schema edits in `/Users/adamsemien/nobc-os/prisma/schema.prisma`.
